feedback.text: I support this act with emphasis on protecting consumer rights in regards to purchasing a video game license. Provided you pay for a video game via a retailer, you should have the right to access the video game no matter what the end result of the service ends up being. If it goes out of business or ceases to provide a service for other reasons, consumers should be entitled to keep playing the game with alternate means, such as an offline-only version or being allowed to host privately run servers independent of the creators and publisher's service.
